sentenceBreakdowns: Sáng (morning) + nay (today) + em (I) + chào (greet) + sư phụ (master)
Chiều (afternoon) + nay (today) + anh (he) + gặp (meet) + sư phụ (master)
Em (I) + gặp (meet) + sư phụ (master) + mỗi (every) + ngày (day)
notes_md:
**sư phụ** = master, the teacher you deeply respect, often in martial arts or traditional crafts. It has a master–disciple vibe, not just a regular teacher.

It’s a noun. You can use it alone to address someone, or with **vị** when counting respectfully (một vị sư phụ). Common pairings: **đệ tử** (disciple), **bái sư** (formally become someone’s disciple), **võ thuật**.

It can also refer to a Buddhist monk in a respectful way.

Close to **thầy**, but *sư phụ* feels more traditional and hierarchical, like a lifelong mentor.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I greet the master.
This afternoon he meets the master.
I meet the master every day.
exampleSentences: Sáng nay em chào sư phụ.
Chiều nay anh gặp sư phụ.
Em gặp sư phụ mỗi ngày.
notes:

It’s a noun. You can use it alone to address someone, or with vị when counting respectfully (một vị sư phụ). Common pairings: đệ tử (disciple), bái sư (formally become someone’s disciple), võ thuật.

It can also refer to a Buddhist monk in a respectful way.

Close to thầy, but sư phụ feels more traditional and hierarchical, like a lifelong mentor.
